name: High-fat low-carbohydrate diet
description: Diet pattern that enhances fat oxidation but impairs high-intensity exercise via downregulated CHO metabolism.

name: n-3 fatty acids
description: Essential omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids; intake reduced when athletes restrict fat below 20% energy. [taiwan_db_key: n3_pufa_total_mg]

name: Alcohol
description: 7 kcal/g; suppresses lipid oxidation, impairs MPS, glycogen storage, rehydration and thermoregulation.

name: Iron
description: Trace mineral; deficiency with or without anemia impairs muscle function and limits work capacity. [taiwan_db_key: iron_per_100g]

name: Vitamin D
description: Fat-soluble vitamin required for calcium/phosphorus metabolism, bone health and skeletal muscle function. [taiwan_db_key: vitamin_d_total_ug]

name: Calcium
description: Mineral central to bone health and muscle/nerve function; intake often suboptimal in athletes restricting energy. [taiwan_db_key: calcium_per_100g]

name: Iron deficiency anemia
description: IDA reduces oxygen-carrying capacity, impairs training adaptation; reversal can take 3-6 months.

name: Hepcidin
description: Iron-regulatory hormone elevated postexercise; interferes with intestinal iron absorption.

name: Antioxidants
description: Nutrients protecting cell membranes from exercise-induced oxidative damage.

name: Vitamin C
description: Ascorbic acid; enhances non-heme iron absorption when consumed with iron-rich plant foods. [taiwan_db_key: vitamin_c_mg]

name: Heme iron
description: Iron from animal foods; well absorbed and recommended for athletes with depleted iron status. [taiwan_db_key: iron_per_100g]

name: Ferritin
description: Acute-phase iron storage protein; serum levels indicate iron status with thresholds 10-35 ng/mL debated.

name: Sports anemia
description: Transient hemoglobin decrease at training initiation due to hemodilution; beneficial adaptation.

name: Stress fracture
description: Bone injury reduced by adequate vitamin D and calcium status, especially in low-EA athletes.

name: 25(OH)D
description: Serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D; insufficiency 20-30 ng/mL, deficiency <20 ng/mL.

name: Type II muscle fiber
description: Fast-twitch fiber whose size increases with adequate vitamin D status.

name: Calcium supplementation
description: 1500 mg/day calcium with 1500-2000 IU vitamin D recommended for athletes with low EA or menstrual dysfunction.

name: Antioxidant supplementation
description: Practice with little evidence of performance benefit and may negatively influence training adaptation.

name: Reactive oxygen species
description: Exercise-induced free radicals serving as signaling molecules for training adaptation.

name: Lipid peroxidation
description: Oxidative damage marker that increases acutely with exercise but is offset by trained antioxidant systems.

name: Pre-event meal
description: Strategy 1-4 h before exercise providing carbohydrate, hydration and gut comfort for performance.

name: Hydration
description: Fluid status maintained before, during and after exercise to support performance and thermoregulation.

name: Sweat
description: Evaporative cooling fluid containing sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium variably across athletes.

name: Hypovolemia
description: Decreased plasma volume from exercise heat stress; causes cardiovascular strain and altered metabolism.

name: Hyperthermia
description: Core body temperature exceeding 40 C; risk of life-threatening heat illness during dehydration.

name: Sodium
description: Major sweat electrolyte; intake supports fluid retention pre- and postexercise. [taiwan_db_key: sodium_per_100g]

name: Magnesium
description: Mineral lost in sweat in small amounts during prolonged exercise. [taiwan_db_key: magnesium_per_100g]

name: Potassium
description: Electrolyte lost in lesser amounts in sweat, important to overall electrolyte balance. [taiwan_db_key: potassium_per_100g]

name: Dehydration
description: Process of losing body water leading to hypohydration; >2% body mass loss impairs cognition and aerobic performance.

name: Urinary specific gravity
description: Hydration status marker; values <1.020 indicate euhydration in midstream first morning sample.

name: Glycerol
description: Plasma expander used for hyperhydration; now prohibited by World Anti-Doping Agency.

name: World Anti-Doping Agency
description: WADA; international body that prohibits glycerol and other plasma expanders for athletes.

name: Sweat rate
description: Variable 0.3-2.4 L/h depending on intensity, environment, fitness; basis for individualized fluid plans.

name: Hyponatremia
description: Plasma sodium <135 mmol/L from overdrinking; symptoms include nausea, seizures and possibly death.

name: Cold beverage
description: 0.5 C drink that may reduce core temperature and improve performance in heat.

name: Muscle cramps
description: Skeletal muscle cramping associated with fatigue, hypohydration, electrolyte imbalance and high salt sweaters.

name: Sodium chloride
description: Dietary salt that aids retention of postexercise rehydration fluids and plasma volume restoration.

name: Caffeine
description: Stimulant; previously thought diuretic but moderate (<180 mg) habitual intake does not cause meaningful losses.

name: Liver glycogen
description: Hepatic carbohydrate store depleted overnight; replenished by pre-event carbohydrate to support blood glucose.

name: Pre-event carbohydrate
description: Intake of 1-4 g/kg in 1-4 h pre-exercise enhances endurance via glycogen and glucose availability.

name: Liquid meal supplement
description: Pre-event option for athletes with nerves or uncertain timetable; quickly digested.

name: Sports beverage
description: Formulated drink delivering carbohydrate (and electrolytes) during exercise, including glucose:fructose blends.

name: Glycogen resynthesis
description: Postexercise refueling at ~5%/h optimized by 1-1.2 g/kg/h carbohydrate during first 4-6 h.

name: Multiple transportable carbohydrates
description: Glucose+fructose blends increasing intestinal absorption and oxidation in prolonged exercise.

name: Recovery nutrition
description: Carbohydrate plus protein strategy enhancing glycogen resynthesis and MPS between sessions.

name: Protein-carbohydrate coingestion
description: Combined intake stimulating MPS during resistance exercise and accelerating recovery between sessions.

name: Delayed onset muscle soreness
description: DOMS; postexercise soreness possibly attenuated by 50-100 g protein during recovery.

name: Dietary supplement
description: Sports nutrition product with variable evidence; subject to contamination, mislabeling, and antidoping risk.

name: NCAA
description: National Collegiate Athletic Association; enforces antidoping regulations including caffeine limits in college sport.

name: Creatine
description: Ergogenic supplement increasing creatine and phosphocreatine; improves repeated high-intensity performance. [taiwan_db_key: creatine]

name: Caffeine
description: Adenosine antagonist supplement reducing fatigue perception; sustains exercise output.

name: Sodium bicarbonate
description: Pre-exercise alkaline supplement increasing extracellular buffering capacity for high-intensity events.

name: Beta-alanine
description: Amino acid precursor that chronically increases muscle carnosine, an intracellular buffer for high-intensity exercise.

name: Nitrate
description: Increases plasma nitrite and nitric oxide production, reducing oxygen cost of exercise; improves nonelite endurance.

name: Beetroot juice
description: Concentrated dietary nitrate source; may cause gut discomfort and red urine discoloration.

name: Carnosine
description: Intracellular buffer increased by chronic beta-alanine supplementation.

name: Nitric oxide
description: Signaling molecule whose production rises with nitrate intake, mediating vascular and metabolic effects.

name: Australian Institute of Sport
description: AIS classification system ranking sports foods/supplements by evidence and safety.

name: Vegetarian diet
description: Plant-based eating pattern; can be adequate but risks low intakes of energy, protein, B12, iron, zinc, calcium.

name: Vitamin B12
description: Vitamin at risk of inadequacy in vegetarian and vegan athletes. [taiwan_db_key: vitamin_b12_ug]

name: Zinc
description: Trace mineral at risk in vegetarian athletes; needed for immune and metabolic function. [taiwan_db_key: zinc_per_100g]

name: Altitude training
description: Daily/intermittent exposure >2000 m to increase red cell mass; requires adequate iron stores.

name: Erythropoiesis
description: Red blood cell production increased by altitude exposure to enhance oxygen carrying capacity.

name: Hot environment
description: Ambient heat exposure exceeding body temperature; impairs evaporative cooling and elevates heat illness risk.

name: Heat exhaustion

name: Exertional heat stroke
description: Body core hyperthermia >40 C; multiorgan dysfunction including brain swelling and CNS abnormalities.

name: Heat acclimatization
description: Adaptive process reducing thermal strain; key strategy for athletes competing in hot environments.

name: Cold environment
description: Exercise setting requiring careful hydration and energy planning to maintain core temperature.

name: Hypothermia

name: Cold-induced diuresis
description: Cold-related fluid loss contributing to hypohydration risk in cold environment exercise.

name: Commission on Dietetic Registration

name: Sports dietitian

name: Body weight assessment

name: Daily carbohydrate intake
description: Recommendation 3-10 g/kg/day (up to 12 g/kg for extreme activities) periodized to training demands.

name: Daily protein intake
description: 1.2-2.0 g/kg/day with regular spacing of 0.3 g/kg doses across the day for muscle adaptation.

name: Pre-event meal
description: Foods/fluids 1-4 h pre-event that restore liver glycogen, ensure hydration and gut comfort.

name: Riboflavin
